title: jamaican pear galette
description: rich, flaky, and fragrant, this monsoon-inspired pear galette is a twist on traditional jamaican cuisine, featuring a long-fermentation-dough and fodmap-friendly ingredients.

ingredients.name: pear puree
ingredients.notes: use ripe but firm pears such as bartlett or anjou.

instructions: in a small bowl, combine warm water, yeast, and sugar. let sit for 5 minutes until frothy.
in a large mixing bowl, combine flour, salt, and yeast mixture.
knead dough for 10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
place dough in greased bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
roll out dough to a thickness of 1/8 inch.
in a small bowl, mix together pear puree, nutmeg, almond extract, eggs, coconut milk, coconut oil, and honey.
spread pear mixture evenly over dough, leaving a 1-inch border around edges.
sprinkle pecans on top, if using.
fold edges of dough up over filling, pressing gently to seal.
place galette on baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 35 minutes.
reduce oven temperature to 350°f and bake for an additional 10-15 minutes until golden brown.
let galette cool for 10 minutes before slicing and serving.
serve warm, topped with additional pear slices or whipped coconut cream, if desired.
